mandatory_rules: agent.md — 8 rules: (1) ABSOLUTE_TRUTH: Never lie or claim something was done when it wasn't. (2) NO_UNAUTHORIZED_CHANGES: Do not modify any code not explicitly mentioned. (3) STOP_ON_ERRORS: If you encounter a problem, stop immediately and report. (4) NEVER_SELF_EDIT: Do not fix anything on your own even if it appears wrong — follow instructions literally. (5) EXPLAIN_AFTER_EACH_STEP: After each step, briefly explain what you did and the result. (6) VERIFY_BEFORE_CHANGING: Read the actual code line by line before applying ANY fix — the consultant may be describing a different version. (7) COMMIT_REPORTING: After every commit, provide commit hash + direct GitHub link. (8) WORKSPACE: /home/z/my-project/revit/
on_critical_finding: STOP_AND_REPORT — do NOT fix independently. Report the finding with exact file, line number, and evidence. Wait for user confirmation before making any change.
verification_protocol: LINE_BY_LINE_BEFORE_CHANGE — Read the target file line by line before applying ANY modification. The consultant may be describing a different version of the code. If the code does not match the consultant's description, flag the discrepancy and do NOT apply the fix.
commit_reporting: HASH_PLUS_GITHUB_LINK — After every commit, provide: (1) Full commit SHA, (2) Direct link: https://github.com/ahmdelbaz28-ux/revit/commit/<SHA>
stop_conditions: Code does not match consultant's description — discrepancy detected
Change would affect file not listed in scope
Consultant fix is based on stale/old version of the code
Self-editing temptation — if something looks wrong but wasn't mentioned, report it instead of fixing it

nfpa72_cross_reference.dead_air_space: §17.6.3.1.3 — detectors must not be in dead air space
nfpa72_cross_reference.voltage_drop: Chapter 10 + NEC 760 — NAC circuit voltage drop (×2 for DC return)
nfpa72_cross_reference.cooking_areas: §17.6.4 — no smoke detectors in cooking areas
